title: Backend: Retention & Storage
description: TTL and size-based retention, compression for sealed chunks. Required before any real usage.

title: TTL-based chunk deletion
description: Delete chunks older than a configured TTL. Per-store retention policy already exists; implement the actual deletion logic.
notes: Closed: Consolidated into 1m5j

title: Size-based retention (keep last N bytes)
description: Evict oldest chunks when a store exceeds a configured byte limit.
notes: Closed: Consolidated into 1m5j

title: Background cleanup goroutine
description: Periodic goroutine that enforces retention policies (TTL and size) across all stores. Must be tracked with sync.WaitGroup for clean shutdown.
notes: Closed: Consolidated into 1m5j

title: Optional compression for sealed chunks
description: Compress sealed chunks with zstd or lz4. Transparent decompression on read.

title: Backend: Ingesters
description: New ingester protocols (OTLP, Fluent Forward, Kafka) and ingester features (backpressure, metrics).

title: OTLP ingester (OpenTelemetry logs, gRPC and HTTP)
description: Accept OpenTelemetry log signals via both gRPC and HTTP OTLP endpoints.

title: Fluent Forward ingester
description: Fluent Bit/Fluentd forward protocol ingester for log pipelines.

title: Kafka consumer ingester
description: Consume logs from Kafka topics for pipeline integration.

title: Backpressure signaling
description: Signal backpressure from chunk managers to ingesters when ingestion is too fast.
notes:
Go's blocking semantics provide basic backpressure for free — if chunk Append blocks, the ingester goroutine stops reading from the network, TCP buffers fill, and the sender slows down.

This issue covers explicit backpressure beyond the implicit kind:
- Return HTTP 429 / RELP NACK instead of just being slow
- Drop low-priority messages under load instead of blocking everything
- Observable metrics on queue depth and backpressure events
- Configurable buffer limits before implicit backpressure kicks in

title: Per-ingester metrics
description: Track ingestion rate, error count, and latency per ingester instance.

title: Backend: Format-Specific Indexes
description: Specialized indexes for structured log formats: JSON, Apache/Nginx, Logfmt.

title: JSON index (top-level fields, nested paths, arrays)
description: Index JSON log records for fast field-based querying. Support top-level fields, nested paths, and array elements.

title: Apache/Nginx access log index
description: Index parsed Apache/Nginx access log fields: method, path, status, bytes, user_agent.

title: Logfmt index
description:
Dedicated logfmt indexer that assumes the entire line is logfmt format.

Unlike the generic KV index (which is conservative to avoid false positives on arbitrary text), this indexer can use a proper logfmt parser that handles:
- Hyphenated keys (pod-name=api-server, container-id=abc123)
- Bare/boolean keys (active without = means true)
- Escape sequences in quoted values (msg="has \"quotes\"")
- Colons and other special characters in keys (service:name=api)

Consider using go-logfmt/logfmt as the parser. The indexer should produce the same index format as the KV index (key=value pairs) so existing query infrastructure works unchanged.

title: Backend: Query Enhancements
description: Aggregations, pattern matching (regex/glob/prefix), and query performance improvements.

title: Aggregation functions (count, count_distinct, min, max)
description: Support count(), count_distinct(), min(), max() on timestamps. Foundation for analytics queries.
close_reason: Subsumed by pipeline design (gastrolog-1oep). Aggregation functions are now part of the stats operator.

title: Group by time buckets
description: Group aggregation results by time buckets for time-series analysis.
close_reason: Subsumed by pipeline design (gastrolog-1oep). Time bucketing is now bin() within stats ... by.

title: Group by attribute values
description: Group aggregation results by attribute values (e.g., group by level, group by source).
close_reason: Subsumed by pipeline design (gastrolog-1oep). Group by attribute values is now stats ... by <field>.

title: Regex support in token search
description: Allow regex patterns in search queries for flexible matching.
close_reason: Implemented regex search with /pattern/ syntax, RE2 engine, full boolean operator support, syntax highlighting, and help docs

title: Prefix/suffix and glob pattern matching
description: Support prefix*, *suffix, and glob patterns in search queries.
close_reason: Implemented glob pattern matching with prefix/suffix/wildcard support, raw-line fallback for dotted identifiers, index acceleration via prefix lookup, and frontend syntax highlighting

title: Query cost estimation in explain

notes: Closed: Cost summary bar added to explain panel with scan estimate, chunk counts, index effectiveness, and reduction percentage.

title: Query timeout enforcement

close_reason: Implemented configurable query timeouts (default 30s) for Search, Histogram, GetContext. Scanner loops check ctx every 1024 iterations. Follow excluded.

Certificate and CA rotation with hot-reload. See docs/cluster_enrollment.md for the full design.

title: Frontend: Polish
description: Responsive design, accessibility (ARIA, keyboard nav, screen readers), and performance optimization (code splitting, bundle size).

title: Responsive design for tablet viewports
description: Optimize layout for tablet-sized screens.

title: Collapsible sidebar for mobile
description: Make sidebar collapsible/hideable on narrow viewports.
notes: Closed: Already implemented — sidebar is already collapsible on narrow viewports.

title: Touch-friendly controls
description: Ensure buttons, toggles, and interactive elements have adequate touch targets.

title: ARIA labels and accessibility
description: Add ARIA labels to interactive elements, ensure full keyboard navigability, focus indicators, screen reader compatibility.
close_reason: Added ARIA labels, keyboard handlers, focus trapping, live regions, focus indicators, and skip link across the frontend

title: Performance optimization (code splitting, bundle size)
description: Profile render performance, implement code splitting by route, optimize bundle size.

title: Frontend code discipline: deduplicate, extract reusable components, improve types
description:
Audit the frontend codebase for code quality improvements:
- Extract repeated patterns into reusable components (e.g. theme-aware class helpers, icon buttons, dropdown/popover pattern)
- Deduplicate similar code across components
- Use TypeScript generics and stricter types where appropriate
- Consolidate inline SVG icons into a shared set
- Clean up arbitrary Tailwind values in favor of canonical classes

title: Retention policies (TTL, size-based, background cleanup)
description:
Implement a retention policy system for stores. A policy defines when chunks should be deleted — by age (TTL) or by total store size (keep last N bytes). A background goroutine periodically evaluates policies and removes expired/excess chunks.

Consolidates:
- 3u5j — Background cleanup goroutine
- 441g — Size-based retention (keep last N bytes)
- 4brs — TTL-based chunk deletion

title: Cron-based chunk rotation
description: Add a cron schedule option to rotation policies. Instead of rotating based on size/age/record count thresholds evaluated at append time, a cron-based policy rotates chunks on a fixed schedule (e.g. every hour, daily at midnight). This requires a background timer rather than the current per-record check in the append path.

title: Backend: Users & Authentication
description: Add user accounts and authentication to the system. User identity enables per-user features like saved queries, preferences, and access control.

title: User registration and login
description: Basic user accounts with email/password registration, login, and session management.
notes:
Keep it simple: username + bcrypt password, SQLite storage.

Registration: username + password → bcrypt hash → store in SQLite.
Login: verify hash → issue JWT (short-lived access token + longer-lived refresh token).
Auth: Authorization header with bearer JWT → verify signature → extract user.
Password change: authenticated user provides old password + new password → verify old → update hash.

No OAuth, no email verification. Those can be layered on later.

JWT plays well with the distributed architecture (yjw5) — headless agents and remote instances can verify tokens without hitting a central session store.

Bootstrap: If no users exist, the UI shows a first-time setup screen to create the initial admin account. Once created, the setup screen disappears and normal login takes over. Must work without CLI access (Docker containers).

title: API authentication (JWT/session tokens)
description:
Authenticate API requests via JWT. Single token (7-day default) verified by signature (no server-side session lookup). Protect endpoints that require a logged-in user. Connect RPC middleware to extract and validate the token on each request.

Token duration is adjustable via the server config in the KV settings store. JWT signing secret is auto-generated during bootstrap and persisted in the KV store so tokens survive backend restarts.

No refresh tokens — simplified to a single long-lived token per the decision in 54xw.

title: User preferences storage
description: Per-user key-value preferences (theme, default store, query settings). Stored server-side and synced to the frontend.

title: Password reset flow
description: Password reset via admin action only. An admin uses the admin API (pid0) to reset a user's password. No email infrastructure required — keeps the system fully self-contained.

title: User management admin API
description: Admin endpoints to list, create, disable, and delete user accounts.
close_reason: Admin API implemented: CreateUser, ListUsers, UpdateUserRole, ResetPassword, DeleteUser. Register locked to first-user-only.

title: No sort direction toggle in follow mode
description: Follow mode always displays newest logs at the top. The forward/reverse sort button is hidden when follow mode is active, so there's no way to switch to oldest-first while following.

title: Unified logging for all chunk rotation events
description: Cron-based rotation logs seal events to stdout, but threshold-based rotation (size, age, record count) happens silently inside ChunkManager.Append() without logging. All rotation events should be logged consistently regardless of trigger.

title: Migrate retention sweeps to shared scheduler
description: Retention currently runs as a per-store goroutine with time.Ticker at a hardcoded 1-minute interval. This should be migrated to the shared Scheduler so retention jobs are visible alongside cron rotation jobs, and the interval becomes configurable via cron expressions rather than hardcoded.

title: Migrate index builds and other ad-hoc tasks to shared scheduler
description:
Index builds are currently managed as unbounded goroutines tracked by indexWg/indexCtx in the orchestrator. These should be migrated to the shared Scheduler using gocron's OneTimeJob support, which would make them visible in ScheduledJobs(), provide built-in concurrency limiting via WithLimitConcurrentJobs, and consolidate shutdown handling.

Beyond index builds, audit the orchestrator for any other ad-hoc goroutine-managed work that could benefit from moving to the scheduler. Candidates might include ingester lifecycle management or any future background tasks.

The Scheduler wrapper will need a new RunOnce method backed by OneTimeJob(OneTimeJobStartImmediately()).

title: Relative time ranges (last=5m) instead of absolute start/end
notes: The sidebar time range presets (5m, 15m, etc.) bake absolute start=/end= timestamps into the query at click time. Re-running the search returns stale results because the time window is frozen. Presets should emit last=5m instead, resolved server-side at query time.

title: Backend: Server Configuration
notes: Service-level configuration exposed via the ConfigService API and editable from the frontend Settings dialog (Service tab). Covers tuning parameters that affect the whole server rather than individual stores/ingesters/policies.

title: Configurable max concurrent scheduler jobs
notes: Expose MaxConcurrentJobs (currently hardcoded default 4 in orchestrator.Config) as a service-level setting. Requires: proto field in GetConfigResponse/a new UpdateServiceConfig RPC, backend config persistence + hot-reload on the orchestrator, and frontend Service tab UI with a number input.

title: Structural JSON index (GIN-style path/containment queries)
notes:
The current JSON extractor flattens shallow JSON into key=value pairs with dot-separated paths. This works for flat/simple JSON logs but falls short for deeply nested structures, arrays of objects, and keys that contain dots.

A proper structural JSON index would support:
- Arrays of objects: spans[*].attributes.db.statement
- Deep nesting beyond 4 levels
- Distinguishing dotted keys (service.name as single key) from nested paths
- Containment queries (does this record's JSON contain this sub-structure)
- Path existence queries

Conceptually similar to PostgreSQL's GIN index for JSONB. Would likely need a new index format and query syntax extensions.

title: RELP ingester (reliable syslog transport)
notes: RELP (Reliable Event Logging Protocol) ingester for receiving logs from rsyslog and other RELP-capable senders. RELP provides reliable delivery over TCP with transaction-based acknowledgements, preventing message loss during network issues or receiver restarts. Commonly used in enterprise syslog infrastructure as a more reliable alternative to plain TCP/UDP syslog.
close_reason: Implemented RELP ingester with shared syslogparse package, end-to-end ack support, tests, and frontend UI

title: File tail ingester (local log file watching)
description:
Native file tailing ingester for watching local log files without requiring external forwarders.

Implementation details:
- Glob patterns for file discovery (param: paths, comma-separated, e.g. /var/log/*.log,/opt/app/logs/*.log)
- fsnotify as primary change detection (both directory-level for new files and file-level for content)
- Slow background poll as safety net for edge cases (param: poll_interval, default 30s, 0 to disable)
- Position bookmarking in <datadir>/state/tail/<ingester_id>.json, persisted across restarts
- Log rotation detection: inode tracking, truncation detection (file shrinks)
- Re-evaluation of glob patterns for new/removed files via fsnotify on parent directories
- Line-delimited: supports both \n and \r\n, each line = one IngestMessage
- SourceTS left as zero (separate issue gastrolog-1n9u handles extraction from content)
- Params: paths (required), poll_interval (default 30s)
acceptance:
- Watches files matching glob patterns and emits one IngestMessage per line
- Supports both \n and \r\n line endings
- Uses fsnotify for primary change detection (new files and content changes)
- Falls back to configurable poll interval as safety net
- Persists file positions in <datadir>/state/tail/<ingester_id>.json
- Resumes from last position after restart
- Detects log rotation (inode change, file truncation) and handles gracefully
- Discovers new files matching globs and stops tracking removed files
- Registered as ingester type 'tail' with factory pattern
notes:
Native file tailing ingester for watching local log files without requiring external forwarders like rsyslog or Promtail. Should support:
- Glob patterns for file discovery (e.g., /var/log/*.log)
- Position bookmarking across restarts
- Log rotation detection (inode tracking, truncation detection)
- Re-evaluation of glob patterns for new/removed files
- Configurable poll interval
close_reason: Implemented tail ingester with fsnotify + doublestar glob support, bookmark persistence, rotation/truncation detection, and frontend configuration UI.

title: Health & Statistics
notes: System health monitoring and statistics. Covers backend metrics collection (ingester throughput, store health, query performance, resource usage) and frontend dashboard to visualize them.

title: Distributed ingestion and query
description: Capabilities built on top of multi-node clustering: federated search across instances, headless agents for remote log collection, shard-based ingestion routing, data replication between nodes, and remote chunk transfer.
notes: Support for running multiple GastroLog instances in a distributed topology. Covers federation (querying across instances), headless agents for remote log collection, cluster coordination, and data replication.
close_reason: All children superseded by multi-node clustering (gastrolog-5foe)

title: Headless agent mode
notes: Lightweight GastroLog instance that tails local files and forwards to a central instance. No UI, minimal footprint, configured via file or flags. Pairs with the file tail ingester (2pr9) on the agent side and any protocol ingester on the central side.
close_reason: Superseded by clustering — nodes join the cluster directly

title: Federated search
notes: Query multiple GastroLog instances from a single UI. A central instance fans out queries to remotes and merges results in timestamp order. Needs a query forwarding API and result stream merging.
close_reason: Superseded by clustering — cross-node queries handled within the cluster

title: Cluster discovery and membership
notes: Instances discover each other and track health. Options: static config, DNS-based, or gossip protocol. Foundation for federated search and shard routing.

title: Data replication
notes: Replicate sealed chunks between instances for redundancy. At least leader-follower replication of sealed chunks. Stretch goal: multi-writer with conflict resolution.
close_reason: Superseded by clustering — Raft handles config replication, record forwarding handles data

title: Shard-based ingestion routing
notes: Route incoming logs to specific instances based on store, source, or consistent hashing. Spreads write load across nodes. Requires cluster membership awareness.
close_reason: Superseded by route entity (gastrolog-4lq9)

title: Remote chunk transfer
notes: API for transferring sealed chunks between instances. Enables rebalancing, migration, cold storage offloading, and data replication. Chunks are immutable once sealed, making transfer straightforward.
close_reason: Superseded by clustering — cross-node data handled by record forwarding (gastrolog-50u6)

title: Server-side KV config store
notes:
Simple key-value store in SQLite for server configuration that doesn't belong in the static config file. Key is text, value is JSON-serialized text. Avoids schema migrations for every new parameter.

Examples of what goes here:
- JWT signing secret (generated on first boot)
- Feature flags
- Runtime-adjustable settings (e.g., max concurrent scheduler jobs)

Queryable from the backend without restart. Distinct from user preferences (2m25), which are per-user — this is system-wide.

title: Server settings UI (Service tab)
description:
Populate the Service tab in the settings dialog with server-level configuration. Currently shows 'No service-level configuration available yet.' placeholder (SettingsDialog.tsx ServiceSettings component).

Initially surfaces auth settings from ServerConfig:
- Token duration (access token lifetime)
- Refresh token duration

JWT secret is NOT exposed in the UI — it's server-internal.

Requires a Settings RPC (or extension to ConfigService) to read/write the server config from the frontend.

Depends on: 54xw (user registration and login — defines the auth settings that need surfacing)

title: Frontend login and registration UI
description:
The backend now enforces auth on all non-public endpoints (2bod). The frontend needs:

1. Auth state management: React context for token storage (localStorage), auth status
2. Transport interceptor: attach Authorization Bearer header to all Connect RPC requests
3. Auth client: instantiate AuthService client in client.ts
4. Registration screen: shown when GetAuthStatus returns needs_setup=true (no users exist)
5. Login screen: shown when user has no valid token but users exist
6. Auth hooks: useLogin, useRegister, useAuthStatus, useLogout
7. Route guard: redirect to login/register when unauthenticated

The frontend should call GetAuthStatus on startup to determine whether to show registration or login. After successful login/register, store the token and attach it to all subsequent requests.

title: Configurable password policy
description: The minimum password length is hardcoded to 8 characters in backend/internal/server/auth.go:51. This should be configurable via server config (e.g. auth.min_password_length). Could also eventually cover other policy options.

title: User menu (account actions dropdown)
description:
Replace the current sign-out icon in the header with a user menu dropdown. Shows the current user's identity and provides account actions:
- Who am I (display current username/role)
- Logout
- Change password
- User preferences (once 2m25 lands)

Compact dropdown triggered by the username or an avatar placeholder, in the same position the sign-out icon currently occupies.
close_reason: Implemented user menu dropdown replacing sign-out icon. Shows username/role, change password, sign out.

title: System Inspector
description: Unified system inspector view accessible from a header icon. Combines store & chunk management (formerly 3ofz) and health & statistics (formerly 1lua) into a single top-level inspector with tabbed panels for chunks, store health, ingester metrics, and dashboard widgets.

title: Inspector view shell (route, layout, tab navigation)
description: Establish the system inspector as a top-level popup accessible from a header icon (next to the settings cog). Set up the modal, tab navigation, and empty placeholder panels. This provides the scaffold that subsequent issues (chunk browser, store health, ingester metrics, etc.) will populate.

title: Click histogram bar segment to filter by level
description: Clicking a colored segment in the histogram should add a level filter to the current query. For example, clicking a red (error) segment appends `level=error` to the search, clicking yellow appends `level=warn`, etc. Clicking the copper (default/no level) segment appends `not level=*` to filter for records with no level attribute. This lets users quickly drill down from the visual overview into specific severity levels.

title: Cycle theme with single icon instead of three buttons
description: Replace the three separate dark/light/system mode icons in the header with a single icon that cycles through the modes on click. Shows the current mode's icon and advances to the next on each click (dark → light → system → dark).

title: TLS support
notes: Add TLS termination to the GastroLog server. Covers certificate management (loading certs from disk, auto-reload on renewal) and enabling HTTPS on the Connect RPC / web endpoint. The HTTP ingester benefits implicitly since it shares the same listener.
close_reason: All child issues completed: certificate management and TLS on HTTP endpoint

title: Certificate management
notes:
Support loading TLS certificate and key from disk (PEM files). Configuration via server flags or settings. Watch for file changes and auto-reload on renewal without restart.

Certificate management needs to be able to handle multiple certificates, which may be used for TLS wrapping the service itself, or in connection with ingesters or storage engines that require client and/or server certificate for authentication.
close_reason: Implemented certificate management with pasted PEM and monitored file paths, security fixes, and UI improvements

title: Enable TLS on HTTP endpoint
notes: Add TLS listener option to the Connect RPC server. When a certificate is configured, serve HTTPS instead of (or alongside) plain HTTP. Includes redirecting HTTP to HTTPS when both are active.
close_reason: Implemented in gastrolog-44h6: HTTPS listener on port+1, HTTP→HTTPS redirect (307), dynamic TLS reconfig

title: Add support for multiple color themes
description:
The frontend already uses CSS custom properties for all colors (~50 tokens), making it straightforward to support multiple themes beyond the current Observatory palette.

Each theme provides its own dark and light variant. The c() helper continues to pick between dark/light — themes just redefine what those colors are by swapping CSS variable values.

Implementation:
- Define theme palettes as CSS variable sets in main.css (e.g., .theme-observatory { ... }, .theme-nord { ... })
- Apply theme class to root element
- Extend Theme type and preferences to store both mode (dark/light/system) and palette
- Add theme picker UI (either in settings or alongside the existing mode toggle)
- Each palette defines both dark and light variants of all ~50 color tokens

title: Timestamp handling improvements
close_reason: All child issues completed: big/small time precision, min/max timestamp tracking, and sealed chunk timestamp indexes

title: Big/small time: store config and header flag for nanosecond vs microsecond timestamp precision
Track IngestTS and SourceTS min/max in unsealed chunk metadata
Ingest and Source timestamp indexes in sealed chunks
Documentation
Fill in README.md
Advanced log handling
description: Features for handling logs that don't fit the simple one-line-per-record model: multi-line events (stack traces, continuation lines), extracting timestamps from message content when the envelope lacks them, and configurable parsing for structured field extraction. Enables better indexing and querying of real-world log formats.

title: Multi-line log events: detect and group stack traces and continuation lines
description: Detect and group multi-line log events into single logical records. Stack traces (e.g. Java, Python, Go), indented continuation lines, and similar patterns span multiple physical lines but represent one event. Current ingestion treats each line as a separate record. Need heuristics or configurable patterns to recognize continuation lines (e.g. lines starting with 'at ', 'Caused by:', whitespace indent) and merge them into one record. Optionally support post-hoc grouping for already-ingested data.

title: Extract SourceTS from log message content when not in envelope
description: When the log envelope (e.g. syslog header, Docker JSON) lacks a timestamp, attempt to extract SourceTS from the message body. Many log formats embed timestamps in the first line (e.g. '2024-01-15 10:30:45 ERROR ...', RFC3339 in JSON). Add configurable parsing (regex, format hints) to extract a timestamp and set SourceTS. Falls back to zero when extraction fails. Complements the SourceTS index work — more records can participate in source-time queries if we can extract timestamps from content.
close_reason: Already implemented by the Timestamp digester

title: Configurable log parsing and field extraction
description: Allow users to define parsers that extract structured fields from log messages at ingest time. Could support regex capture groups, JSON path extraction, or format-specific parsers (e.g. common log format). Extracted fields would populate record attributes for indexing and querying. Enables key=value queries on ad-hoc fields without requiring the log source to emit structured data. May integrate with digester pipeline or a new parsing stage.

title: Docker logs ingester
description: Ingester that pulls logs from Docker containers. Docker logs are thoroughly timestamped, so we can rely on the Docker timestamp as IngestTS (no need for a separate receive-time timestamp). Must support client/server certificates for authentication (mTLS).
notes: Backend implementation complete. Frontend still needs Docker ingester support in the ingester configuration UI.

title: Chatterbox: multi-record formatter for stack dumps and help output
description: Expand the chatterbox ingester with a formatter that turns content like stack dumps or command help output (that has accidentally found its way into logs) into multiple separate records. Each line or logical unit becomes its own record—not a single multi-line record. Useful for content that was concatenated into one log entry but should be queried and displayed as discrete items.

title: Store Management
description: Backend and tooling for store lifecycle: deletion, migration between types, and related operations.

title: Delete store
description: Ability to delete a store. Must handle: removing config, deleting chunk data and indexes, cleanup of any store-specific resources. May need confirmation or safeguards (e.g. store must be empty, or explicit force flag).

title: Migrate between store types
description: Tooling or workflow to migrate chunk data from one store type to another (e.g. file → memory for testing, or consolidating stores). Involves reading from source store, writing to destination, updating config.

title: Export store
description: Dump store data (chunks + indexes) to an archive for backup or portability.

title: Import store
description: Restore a store from an export archive.

title: Clone store
description: Copy a store (config + data) to a new store ID. Useful for staging or testing.

title: Reindex store
description: Rebuild all indexes for a store (e.g. after corruption or when adding new index types).

title: Validate store
description: Check chunk and index integrity for a store.

title: Compact / vacuum store
description: Reclaim space for file stores—remove deleted chunks, consolidate.

title: Rename store
description: Change store ID. Must update filters, ingester routes, and any references.

title: Merge stores
description: Combine two stores into one.

title: Decommission store
description: Stop new ingestion but allow in-flight work to complete before deletion or migration.

title: Detailed store stats
description: Expose detailed stats: chunk count, record count, disk usage, index sizes per store.

title: Pause / resume store ingestion
description: Temporarily stop or resume ingestion for a store.

title: Prevent nested store directories (file store)
description: Validate that a file store's data directory is not nested inside another store's directory (e.g. /data/store1 and /data/store1/archive). Prevents recursive deletion, locking conflicts, and confusion. Reject or warn at config time.

title: Help system harness
description: Infrastructure for contextual help: direct navigation to relevant docs from any UI context (e.g. [?] opens storage engine help when configuring a store). Support for doc IDs, routing, and embeddable help panels or dialogs.
