- **Plan explainer:** Show which node each vault lives on, visualize route topology across nodes (ingesting node → forwarding → owning node)
- **Search:** Allow searching across remote vaults (query forwarding, similar to record forwarding)
- **Dashboard/stats:** Aggregate vault stats from all nodes into a unified cluster view

Depends on cross-node record forwarding (gastrolog-50u6) being in place.

title: Inspector rework: togglable node/entity organization
description: Rework the inspector panel from 5 separate tab panels (Vaults, Ingesters, Jobs, Metrics, Cluster) into a unified two-mode layout with a togglable Nodes/Entities view. Nodes mode shows per-node entity sections; Entities mode groups items by node. Single-node mode hides the toggle. Includes extracting reusable card components (VaultCard, IngesterCard, JobCard, SystemStatsView) and a shared Badge component.

title: Forwarded records skip post-seal work (compression + indexing)
description: Records arriving via cluster ForwardRecords RPC use Orchestrator.Append which calls cm.Append directly, bypassing seal detection. When the chunk manager rotates during append (age/size policy), no onSeal callback fires, so neither compression nor index build jobs are scheduled. Local ingest uses a separate path (Vault.Append) with proper seal detection. Fix: unify into a single appendRecord path used by all record sources.

title: Fix react-doctor regression: 84 → target 96+
description: react-doctor score dropped from ~96 to 84 across recent feature work (cluster-awareness, cross-node forwarding, etc.). 35 errors across: React Compiler can't optimize (BigIntLiteral, ref-during-render, setState-in-effect), derived state in useEffect. Need to fix all errors and reduce warnings to recover the score.

title: Refactor main.go into internal/app composition root
description: main.go has grown to 2021 lines. It should be ~100 lines handling only Cobra CLI parsing, logging setup, and signal trapping. All composition/wiring logic should move to an internal/app package.

title: Update README with clustering setup and current feature set
description:
The README is missing documentation for multi-node clustering, which is now a core feature. It also doesn't reflect several flags added since the initial write.

### What to add

**Clustering section** covering:
- Every raft server auto-bootstraps as a single-node cluster (always-cluster mode) with auto-generated TLS for inter-node communication
- CLI flags: `--cluster-addr` (default `:4565`), `--join-addr`, `--join-token`, `--voteless`
- CLI join flow: start node2 with `--join-addr <node1-cluster-addr> --join-token <token>`
- Runtime join from the UI: Settings > Nodes > "Join Cluster" (available when running as a single-node cluster)
- Multi-node Docker Compose example

**Server flags table** — add the missing flags:

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--cluster-addr` | Cluster gRPC listen address | `:4565` |
| `--join-addr` | Leader's cluster address to join | (none) |
| `--join-token` | Join token from the leader node | (none) |
| `--voteless` | Join as a non-voter (read replica) | `false` |

**Other updates:**
- `--cluster-init` is deprecated (auto-bootstrap replaced it) — don't document it
- Review feature list for anything else that's drifted

title: Settle on a convention for entity filtering in queries
description:
The `vault=` query directive only accepts UUIDs but its name suggests it could accept a vault name. The help docs incorrectly say `vault=NAME` with an example `vault=prod`. Meanwhile, record attributes use explicit suffixes: `ingester_id`, `node_id`.

This needs a consistent convention:

1. **Name-based filtering** — resolve `vault=prod` to the vault's UUID via config lookup. More user-friendly, especially for manual queries. Requires passing a config store into the query parser.
2. **Rename to `vault_id=`** — consistent with `ingester_id`/`node_id`. Breaking change for existing saved queries.
3. **Support both** — `vault=` resolves by name, `vault_id=` by UUID. Most flexible but adds complexity.

Same question applies to `chunk=` and `pos=` (currently ID-based). And should `ingester_id=` and `node_id=` also support name lookups (e.g. `node_name=`)?

title: S3, Azure Blob Store, and GCS vault types
description:
Add cloud object storage backends (S3, Azure Blob Store) as sealed-chunk-only vault types for archival and compliance retention.

## Design

### Vault type classification

Introduce a trait-based distinction between vault types:
- **Appendable** (file, memory) — accepts live records via the filter/route pipeline
- **Sealed-only** (s3, azure) — only receives complete chunks, typically via migration from an appendable vault

### Migration path

Cloud vaults don't accept live writes. Chunks arrive via migration from a paired appendable vault:
1. File vault seals a chunk
2. Chunk is converted to the cloud format and uploaded
3. Original chunk can be deleted by retention

This makes cloud vaults a retention tier that preserves data instead of deleting it.

### Merged file format

The current file vault uses 3 separate files (raw log, attributes, index). Cloud chunks merge everything into a single self-contained blob. Indexes are omitted — queries scan the full blob.

Format design considerations:
- **Record framing**: length-prefixed records for safe streaming reads
- **Compression**: whole-blob zstd (best ratio for log data) or block-level (enables partial reads via range requests)
- **Header metadata**: time range, record count, attribute summary — stored both in the blob header and as object metadata (S3 tags / Azure blob metadata) so the query engine can skip blobs without downloading
- **Attribute encoding**: shared string table (dictionary encoding) for repeated keys like `host`, `level`, `ingester_id`

### Query path

Queries against cloud vaults are inherently slower than local file vaults. Strategies to mitigate:
- Time-range filtering via header metadata (skip blobs without downloading)
- Aggressive rotation before migration (keep individual blobs small)
- Accept that cloud vaults are for archival, not real-time search

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
## Object metadata for query-time filtering

Both S3 and Azure Blob Store support key-value metadata on individual objects:
- **S3**: user-defined metadata headers (`x-amz-meta-*`) + object tags
- **Azure Blob**: up to 8KB of user-defined metadata

Store chunk summary info as object metadata on upload:
```
x-amz-meta-time-start: 1709424000000000000
x-amz-meta-time-end:   1709427600000000000
x-amz-meta-records:    84521
x-amz-meta-bytes-raw:  127364088
```

The query engine can then do time-range filtering via `HeadObject` or `ListObjects` (S3 returns metadata in list responses) without downloading any blob data. For a vault with thousands of archived chunks, this is the difference between thousands of HTTP round-trips vs. a single list call.

The same metadata also lives in the blob's binary header — the object-level metadata is a fast-path cache to avoid downloading anything when the time range doesn't match.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
## Open questions (from gastrolog-j9oq)

- **SDK/library selection**: Which Go SDKs for S3 and Azure Blob? AWS SDK v2 (github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) and Azure Blob SDK (github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob) are the obvious choices — need to evaluate API ergonomics, streaming upload/download support, and retry behavior.
- **Cost implications**: Document expected cost model (per-GB storage, per-request pricing, egress fees on query reads). This affects design decisions like blob size, metadata query strategy, and whether aggressive time-range filtering is a hard requirement vs. nice-to-have.
- **Local cache layer**: Should there be a local disk cache in front of remote storage for recently-queried blobs? Repeated queries against the same archived chunk would otherwise incur repeated downloads and egress costs.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text: Add GCS (Google Cloud Storage) as a third cloud vault type. Same sealed-only semantics as S3 and Azure. Go SDK: cloud.google.com/go/storage. GCS supports object metadata for time-range filtering. Consider using a unified interface (e.g. gocloud.dev/blob) to reduce per-provider boilerplate.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text: GCS metadata: supports up to 8KB of custom key-value metadata per object, set at upload and returned in list/stat responses. Same time-range filtering strategy as S3 (x-amz-meta-*) and Azure blob metadata applies directly — no design changes needed for the third provider.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
## Revised design: single `cloud` vault type with BlobStore abstraction

### Why not gocloud.dev/blob
gocloud.dev/blob's `ListObject` strips custom metadata, forcing per-object `Attributes()` calls. All three providers natively return metadata in their list APIs (S3 `ListObjectsV2`, GCS `Objects.List`, Azure `ListBlobs`). A custom abstraction preserves this.

### BlobStore interface

```go
type BlobStore interface {
    Upload(ctx context.Context, key string, data io.Reader, metadata map[string]string) error
    Download(ctx context.Context, key string) (io.ReadCloser, error)
    Delete(ctx context.Context, key string) error
    List(ctx context.Context, prefix string) ([]BlobInfo, error)
}

type BlobInfo struct {
    Key      string
    Size     int64
    Metadata map[string]string
}
```

Three implementations (~100-150 lines each), each using the provider's native list-with-metadata.

### Vault config shape

Single vault type `cloud`, provider selected via params:
```
type: cloud
params:
  provider: s3|azure|gcs
  bucket: gastrolog-vaults
  region: us-east-1
  endpoint: http://localhost:9000  # optional, for emulators
  prefix: node-1/                  # optional key prefix
```

### Object metadata stored per chunk
- `time-start`: earliest ingest timestamp (nanoseconds)
- `time-end`: latest ingest timestamp (nanoseconds)
- `records`: record count
- `bytes-raw`: uncompressed data size
- `vault-id`: source vault UUID
- `chunk-id`: chunk UUID

title: Expand storage help with routing, rotation/retention strategies, and cold storage guidance
description:
The current `storage.md` is a brief overview (20 lines) that lists vault types and links to sub-pages. It's missing:

1. **Record routing** — how records flow from filters to vaults. The current help system has a "Filtering" page that explains filter expressions, but nothing about the routing step that connects filters to vaults. The `routes.md` topic doesn't exist (Filters and Routes both link to `routing` which only covers filtering). Routes deserve their own section explaining how a route binds a filter + digester pipeline to one or more destination vaults.

2. **Rotation strategies** — guidance on choosing rotation policy parameters. When should you use time-based vs. size-based vs. cron rotation? What chunk sizes work well? What are the trade-offs between many small chunks (faster index builds, more overhead) vs. fewer large chunks (slower sealing, bigger scans of the active chunk)?

3. **Retention strategies** — guidance on combining TTL, size budget, and chunk count. How do they interact (union semantics)? Common patterns: compliance (TTL-only), budget-constrained (maxBytes), rolling window (maxChunks).

4. **Cold/slow storage tiers** — once S3/Azure vaults land (gastrolog-54ml), the storage page needs to explain the tiered model: appendable vaults for live data, sealed-only vaults for archival. Migration paths, query performance trade-offs, and when to use each tier.

title: MQTT subscribing ingester
description:
Add an ingester type that subscribes to an MQTT broker and ingests messages as log records.

## Design

- Connects to an MQTT broker (v3.1.1 / v5.0) and subscribes to one or more topic patterns
- Each incoming MQTT message becomes a log record
- Topic is stored as a record attribute (e.g. `mqtt_topic`)
- Standard ingester params: `addr` (broker URL), `topics` (comma-separated or list), `client_id`, `qos`
- Optional TLS and authentication (username/password, client certificates)
- Supports wildcard subscriptions (`+` single-level, `#` multi-level)

## Use cases

- IoT device logs published to MQTT topics
- Edge/embedded systems that use MQTT as their primary transport
- Bridging MQTT event streams into GastroLog for search and analysis

title: Add 'Test connection' button to ingester config panels
description:
Add a test-connection capability for ingesters that connect to external services. When configuring an ingester, a 'Test connection' button should attempt a quick connect-and-disconnect using the current params and report success/failure inline.

Applicable ingesters: mqtt, kafka, syslog (TCP mode), relp, fluentfwd, http, otlp, docker.

Not applicable: tail (local file), chatterbox (synthetic), metrics (internal).

Requires:
- Backend: new RPC endpoint (e.g. TestIngesterConnection) that instantiates the ingester with the given params, attempts connection with a short timeout, and returns success or error message.
- Frontend: 'Test connection' button in each applicable ingester form, with loading/success/error states.

Motivation: MQTT v3/v5 protocol mismatch produced a silent EOF with no actionable feedback in the UI. A test button would surface this immediately during configuration.

title: HTTPS port collides with cluster gRPC on default settings
description:
deriveHTTPSPort() returns HTTP port + 1 (e.g. 4565), which is always occupied by the cluster gRPC listener (--cluster-addr defaults to :4565). Enabling TLS in Settings > Certificates will fail with a bind error because the cluster listener is already bound to that port.

Every node always runs a Raft cluster (even single-node), so this isn't an edge case — it affects any user who enables TLS.

Options:
1. Change HTTPS default to HTTP port + 2 (4566), leaving 4565 for cluster
2. Make deriveHTTPSPort() aware of the cluster port and skip it
3. Move cluster gRPC to a different default (breaking change, less ideal)

title: Cluster leader election events not logged on surviving nodes
description: Raft internal logger was set to io.Discard, making election events invisible. Observer used non-blocking mode which can silently drop observations. Fix: wire hclog→slog adapter for Raft internals, switch observer to blocking mode, filter noisy entering-follower-state messages.

title: Rethink click interactions on log records
description:
Currently, clicking anywhere on a record's text copies the clicked value to the search bar. This is frustrating when the intent is simply to select a record and view its details pane.

Proposed interaction model:
- **Click** — select the record (open/focus the details pane), no search bar side-effect
- **Shift+click** on a field value — add it as a filter to the search bar (e.g. `container_name="web-1"`)
- **Cmd+click** (or Ctrl+click) — copy the raw value to clipboard
- **Double-click** on text — standard text selection (browser default)

The current behavior makes the details pane awkward to use because every click pollutes the search bar.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text: Additional idea: in the Details pane message body, Cmd+click on a value inside a JSON structure could insert the JSON path as a search filter (e.g. `body.request.method="GET"`). This would make drilling into structured logs much faster than manually typing paths.

title: Details pane sluggish with large JSON messages
description: The details pane becomes noticeably slow when rendering large messages (~37KB+). Likely caused by syntax highlighting and JSON formatting being applied eagerly to the entire body. Should investigate virtualizing the rendered output, lazy highlighting, or capping the initially rendered portion with an expand-on-demand pattern.

title: Internal slog ingester: capture GastroLog's own logs into the pipeline
description:
Add a self-log ingester that hooks into the slog handler chain and feeds GastroLog's own log output back into the ingest pipeline as structured JSON records. This makes internal logs searchable, persistent, and visible in the timeline alongside application logs.

Implementation:
- Custom slog.Handler wrapper (logging/capture.go) that tees records to a bounded channel, skipping pipeline components (orchestrator, ingester, chunk, digest) to prevent feedback loops
- New ingester type 'selflog' that reads from the capture channel and emits IngestMessages with structured JSON body and slog attrs mapped to ingest attrs
- Standard factory + wiring in app.go
- Frontend form (minimal — maybe just an on/off, no real params needed)
- Help page

Records are JSON-encoded with all slog attributes preserved for structured querying (e.g. component=raft AND level>=WARN).

title: Fix react-doctor audit warnings (87 → 95+)
description:
The react-doctor audit reports 5 errors and 163 warnings across 53 files. Actionable categories:

- **5 errors**: React Compiler can't optimize try/catch blocks (ServiceSettings, SetupWizard, AuthPage, UsersSettings, FiltersSettings)
- **14 warnings**: Array index used as key — causes bugs on reorder/filter
- **10 warnings**: Clickable elements missing keyboard event listeners
- **11 warnings**: Static HTML elements with click handlers missing ARIA roles
- **7 warnings**: Components with 7+ useState calls — consider useReducer
- **7 warnings**: Components over 500 lines — consider splitting
- **3 warnings**: useState initialized from prop — derive during render instead
- **1 warning**: dangerouslySetInnerHTML in Mermaid.tsx
- **1 warning**: Sequential awaits in SetupWizard (use Promise.all)
- **2 warnings**: Inline render functions in HelpDialog
- **2 warnings**: Duplicate exports in icons.tsx

Not actionable (ignore): unused exports/types in generated protobuf files, unused fsm_pb.ts (generated), barrel re-exports from hooks/index.ts.

title: Backend code quality improvements
description: Comprehensive backend health check revealed several issues across concurrency, error handling, resource management, architecture, and test coverage. This epic tracks all fixes needed to bring the backend to pristine shape.

title: Convert panic in NewOrchestrator to returned error
description: NewOrchestrator() at orchestrator/orchestrator.go:198 panics on scheduler creation failure. Constructors should never panic — return an error instead so callers can handle it gracefully. This is a crash risk in production.

title: Convert panic in findGroup to returned error
description: findGroup() at cmd/gastrolog/cli/settings.go:260 panics at runtime on unknown settings group name. This is called from CLI code, not init() — should return an error instead of crashing the process.

title: Add context timeouts to config store loads in server.go
description: server.go uses context.Background() for config store operations at lines 365, 388, 484, 515. If the config store blocks, these hang indefinitely, potentially deadlocking request handlers. Wrap with context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second).

title: Reduce mutex scope in chunk manager doAppend
description: doAppend() at chunk/file/manager.go:235-249 holds the mutex during file open, encoding, and file write — serializing all record appends. Refactor to: (1) hold lock to snapshot active state, (2) release for I/O, (3) re-lock to update counters. This is the main ingest throughput bottleneck under load.

title: Make broadcast subscriber dispatch async
description: dispatch() at cluster/broadcast.go:47-54 calls all subscriber callbacks synchronously while holding a read lock. If any callback blocks or is slow, it blocks the RPC handler and all other subscribers. Dispatch callbacks asynchronously or use a buffered worker pool.

title: Use errors.Join in mmapCursor.Close
description: mmapCursor.Close() at chunk/file/record_reader.go:298 aggregates multiple close errors but only returns the first one — all subsequent errors are silently lost. Use errors.Join() (Go 1.20+) to preserve all errors.

title: Extract ingester type registry to avoid hardcoded switch
description: TestIngester() at server/config_ingesters.go:249-265 has a hardcoded switch on ingester type names that must be manually kept in sync with buildFactories() in app.go:668-679. Extract a shared registry or scan the factories map dynamically to eliminate the duplication.

title: Protect record forwarder backoff state from data race
description: In cluster/record_forwarder.go:266-269, logFailure() reads nf.failures after releasing rf.mu, but the backoff fields are modified inside the flushLoop goroutine. This is technically a data race. Either protect backoff state with its own mutex, or copy to a temporary before releasing the lock.

title: Add error wrapping to bare returns in chunk/file
description:
Several functions in chunk/file/ lose error context by returning bare errors:
- manager.go:174 (MkdirAll), manager.go:181 (OpenFile), manager.go:213 (loadExisting)
- record_reader.go:23-26 (loadDict)
- server/query.go:700, 1164

Wrap with fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) so stack traces are meaningful.

title: Document notify.Signal.C() contract and add concurrency tests
description:
notify.Signal at notify/signal.go has zero tests. The C() method returns a channel reference without holding the lock (line 27-32) — callers must re-call C() after each receive, but this contract is undocumented. Add:
1. Documentation of the caller contract
2. TestSignalConcurrentNotify with t.Parallel() + -race
3. Verify no panics, all waiters woken, no missed notifications

title: Add tests for app.Run startup sequence
description:
internal/app has 2.1K LOC and zero tests. The entire startup sequence — loadLocalConfig(), finalizeNodeSetup(), awaitReplication(), startClusterServices() — is untested. Add:
1. Unit tests for individual startup functions
2. Integration test for single-node startup happy path
3. Error path tests for bootstrap failures

title: Add tests for configDispatcher error paths
description:
configDispatcher.Handle() in app/dispatch.go silently logs errors from AddVault, RemoveIngester, LoadServerSettings without propagating them. No tests verify:
- What happens when AddVault fails mid-operation (inconsistent state?)
- What happens when vault is already registered
- Whether errors are logged correctly
Mock failures and verify state consistency + logging.

title: Add sysmetrics edge case tests
description:
internal/sysmetrics (121 LOC) has zero tests and uses package-level mutable state. Edge cases to test:
- CPUPercent() called before first init (lastWall is zero)
- System clock goes backward (negative CPU%)
- Concurrent access to global state
- wall <= 0 fallback path

title: Add t.Parallel to independent subtests across backend
description:
Zero uses of t.Parallel() across 114 test files. Independent tests run sequentially, slowing CI and missing concurrency bugs. Convert independent tests to use t.Parallel() + t.Run(), especially in:
- orchestrator tests
- cluster tests
- ingester tests
- config store tests

title: Replace time.Sleep waits with proper synchronization in tests
description:
15+ tests use time.Sleep() with hardcoded waits that are flaky on slow CI:
- cluster_test.go: 500ms for leader election
- jobtracker_test.go: 50ms for job completion
- docker/ingester_test.go: 100-200ms sleeps

Replace with context.WithTimeout + polling, channels, or atomics for synchronization.

title: Add benchmarks for hot paths: scanner, KV lookups, tokenization
description:
Only 11 benchmarks exist. Missing benchmarks for performance-critical paths:
- query/scanner.go: Record iteration (critical for search)
- index/file/kv/indexer.go: KV index lookups
- tokenizer/token.go: Tokenization (used in indexing)
- server/compress.go: Response compression (pooled writers)
- chunk/file/manager.go: Sealed chunk index caching

These benchmarks are needed to detect performance regressions.

title: Add error path tests for server, orchestrator, and ingesters
description:
Only 2 test functions explicitly test error/deny scenarios. Missing critical error path tests:
- Server: streaming RPC cancellation, vault operation failures, proxy query forwarding errors
- Orchestrator: config reload failure, ingester crash recovery, scheduler queue overflow
- Ingesters: missing required params, connection timeouts, rate limiting edge cases

Error paths are where bugs hide.

title: Add cluster failure scenario tests
description:
No cluster-level integration tests exist for failure scenarios:
- Network partition (leader unreachable)
- Raft log replication errors
- Multi-node config divergence
- Node eviction & recovery
- Membership changes (join/leave)

These are the hardest bugs to find in production.

title: Make QueryServer.remoteSearcher and VaultServer.remoteForwarder constructor params
description: QueryServer and VaultServer use post-construction setters (SetRemoteSearcher at server/query.go:58-62, SetRemoteForwarder at server/vault.go:61-63) to break circular dependencies. These fields can be nil if the setter is never called. Make the fields unexported and document the circular dependency reason. Consider a lazy-init pattern or two-phase construction protocol.

title: Deduplicate ingester ParamDefaults registration
description: In app.go:buildFactories(), the same 11 ingester types are registered twice — once for factories (lines 668-679) and once for defaults (lines 681-693). If a new ingester is added to one but not the other, behavior silently diverges. Each ingester package should return a struct with both factory + defaults, or use a single registration call.

title: Frontend code quality improvements
description: Comprehensive frontend health check revealed issues across React patterns, architecture, performance, accessibility, and test coverage. This epic tracks all fixes needed to bring the frontend to pristine shape. react-doctor score: 90/100.

title: Fix stale closure in useSearch useCallback
description: useSearch.ts line 117 captures old state.records inside useCallback (dep array line 208 includes state.records and state.resumeToken). When append is true, it spreads the stale captured records: `const allRecords = append ? [...state.records] : []`. This can cause search results from the wrong query to appear. Fix: use setState updater function to access current state instead of closing over it.

title: Fix stale closure in useFollow connectStream
description: useFollow.ts line 171-172: connectStream has empty dependency array [], but scheduleReconnect (line 190) calls the stale version of connectStream. On reconnect after a network drop, follow mode uses stale state. Mitigated by refs but fragile. Fix: use a ref for connectStream or restructure the reconnect logic.

title: Fix React Compiler blocker in SetupWizard try/catch
description: SetupWizard.tsx line 184: React Compiler reports 'can't optimize this code — Todo: Support value blocks (conditional, logical, optional chaining, etc) within a try/catch statement'. This is the only compiler error in the codebase. Restructure the try/catch to avoid value blocks inside it (extract expressions before the try or use intermediate variables).

title: Lazy-load Mermaid library (481KB eager load)
description: Mermaid.tsx line 2 imports mermaid eagerly via `import mermaid from 'mermaid'`. At 481KB it's the largest chunk in the bundle, but only used in HelpDialog (a modal). HelpDialog already lazy-loads its Markdown component but not Mermaid. Fix: dynamically import mermaid inside MermaidDiagram's useEffect.

title: Decompose SearchView.tsx (1,201 lines) into focused components
description:
SearchView.tsx is a god component managing query state, search execution, follow mode, histogram, pagination, panel layout, dialog state, context fetching, and field aggregation. 12+ useState/useRef in the first 400 lines. Extract into:
- SearchResultsPanel (records display, pagination, selection)
- SearchControls (query input, sidebar, filters)
- DialogManager (plan, history, preferences, settings)
This will improve testability, reduce cognitive load, and help the React Compiler optimize each piece independently.

title: Add list virtualization for log entries
description: SearchView.tsx lines 1114-1145: displayList.map() renders every record in the DOM even if off-screen. With auto-refresh polling, DOM grows unbounded — 10K records = 10K DOM nodes causing sluggish scroll, layout thrashing, and memory bloat. Add @tanstack/react-virtual or react-window for windowed rendering. Only visible rows + small overscan should be in the DOM.

title: Add role and keyboard handlers to clickable non-interactive elements
description:
7 clickable non-interactive elements lack keyboard event listeners, 6 lack role attributes. Files:
- QueryHistory.tsx:50
- ExpandableCard.tsx:40
- SavedQueries.tsx:104
- LogEntry.tsx:90, 105, 118
- VaultCard.tsx:180
Fix: add role='button' + onKeyDown (Enter/Space) or convert to <button> elements.

title: Extract shared severity constants
description:
Severity color/label maps defined in 3+ places:
- BADGE_STYLE in LogEntry.tsx:14-20
- severityStyles in SearchSidebar.tsx:76-102
- SEVERITY_COLOR_MAP in HistogramChart.tsx
Extract to a shared constants/severity.ts and import everywhere. Currently if a severity level is added, 3 files must be updated.

title: Type TanStack Router search params to eliminate as-any casts
description: SearchView.tsx has 7+ `as any` casts (lines 291, 509, 523, 892, 971, 1067, 1191) mostly for TanStack Router search param mismatches. Type the router search validation properly so navigate() and search params flow through without unsafe casts. Silent type holes allow bugs to ship undetected.

title: Fix useState initialized from props in TimeRangePicker
description: TimeRangePicker.tsx lines 36-37: useState initialized from props rangeStart/rangeEnd. If the parent changes these props, the internal state doesn't update — it diverges. Also VaultsSettings.tsx:74. Fix: derive during render instead of storing in state, or use a key prop to reset.

title: Reduce prop drilling in DetailSidebar and SearchSidebar
description: DetailSidebar.tsx passes 28 props, most just forwarded to DetailPanelContent. SearchSidebar.tsx has 13 props, many are callbacks. This creates coupling and makes refactoring harder. Consider a DetailPanelContext for interaction callbacks (onFieldSelect, onChunkSelect, onVaultSelect, onPosSelect, onContextRecordSelect).

title: Add form label associations (htmlFor/id) to FormField
description: FormField.tsx wraps with <label> but doesn't use htmlFor. Input fields lack id attributes. Screen readers can't associate labels with inputs. Fix: add id to every input and htmlFor to every label. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A requirement.

title: Memoize aggregateFields call in SearchView
description: SearchView.tsx lines 650-651: aggregateFields() is O(n*m) where n=records, m=field cardinality. Called on every render — any state change re-aggregates the entire dataset. With 10K records and 50 fields, this is significant work on every click or scroll. The result is deterministic from displayRecords — should only recompute when records change.

title: Use Promise.all for parallel awaits in SetupWizard
description: SetupWizard.tsx line 191: 4 sequential await statements that appear independent. Use Promise.all() for parallel execution. react-doctor flagged this.

title: Break down large components flagged by react-doctor
description:
7 components flagged as too large (321+ lines):
- SetupWizard.tsx:64 (321 lines)
- CertificatesSettings.tsx:111
- HistogramChart.tsx:91
- ServiceSettings.tsx:36
- HelpDialog.tsx:52
- SearchView.tsx:115 (covered by gastrolog-5xs8)
- VaultsSettings.tsx:31
Extract logical sections into focused sub-components.

title: Convert high-useState components to useReducer
description:
7 components flagged with 5+ useState calls:
- HistogramChart.tsx:107 (7 useState!)
- ServiceSettings.tsx:36
- HelpDialog.tsx:52
- TimeRangePicker.tsx:33
- SearchView.tsx:115
- VaultsSettings.tsx:31
- RoutesSettings.tsx:23
Group related state with useReducer for clearer state transitions and easier debugging.

title: Extract inline render functions in HelpDialog
description: HelpDialog.tsx lines 349, 355: inline render functions renderSidebarContent() prevent proper React reconciliation. Extract to named components for stable identity across renders.

title: Clean up unused exports and types
description: react-doctor found 86 unused exports (addToast, various proto re-exports, component exports) and 15 unused types (SuggestRule, etc.). Also 1 unused file: fsm_pb.ts. Clean up to reduce bundle noise and dead code.

title: Add MSW mock server for API hook testing
description: All 28 API hooks in src/api/hooks/ are untested because there's no way to mock the Connect RPC backend. Add MSW (Mock Service Worker) to intercept network requests in tests. This unblocks testing for useAuth, useConfig, useFollow, useVaults, useSearch, and all other data-fetching hooks.

title: Add tests for SearchView core user flows
description:
SearchView.tsx (45.3K, 1201 lines) has zero tests. It's the core app feature managing search, follow mode, pagination, and field aggregation. Add tests for:
1. Query input → search execution → results display
2. Record selection → detail panel population
3. Follow mode toggle → streaming state
4. Auto-refresh polling behavior
5. Pagination (load more) trigger
Depends on MSW mock server for API mocking.

title: Add tests for settings pages
description:
The 5 largest settings pages are completely untested:
- VaultsSettings.tsx (24.0K)
- ServiceSettings.tsx (22.6K)
- CertificatesSettings.tsx (15.3K)
- NodesSettings.tsx (15.2K)
- RoutesSettings.tsx (11.2K)
All config management flows — create, edit, delete, validation — lack coverage. Test at least CRUD operations and validation for each.

title: Replace CSS class assertions with behavior checks in tests
description:
Existing component tests are brittle — they assert on CSS class names:
- Checkbox.test.tsx:58 expects 'bg-copper'
- ExpandableCard.test.tsx:83 expects 'text-copper'
- Buttons.test.tsx:32 expects 'bg-copper'
These break on any style refactor. Replace with behavior assertions: check aria-checked, disabled state, visibility, text content — not CSS classes.

title: Add tests for untested hooks (16 of 23 untested)
description:
16 of 23 custom hooks have no tests:
- useDialogState (dialog visibility + inspector glow, 800ms timeout)
- usePipelineFields (debounced RPC, 300ms, AbortController)
- usePanelLayout (responsive behavior, auto-collapse)
- useValidation (form validation)
- useTimeRange (time range state)
- useHelp (help context)
- useThemeSync (cross-tab sync)
- useMediaQuery (responsive breakpoints)
- useExpandedCards (card expansion)
Plus all API hooks (covered by separate MSW issue).
Priority: usePipelineFields and useDialogState have timing behaviors that are most bug-prone.

title: Add keyboard and accessibility tests
description:
No keyboard interaction tests exist. No axe-core/jest-axe integration. Missing tests for:
- Dialog escape-to-close
- Form tab order
- Dropdown arrow key navigation
- ARIA roles and labels
- Screen reader announcements
Add jest-axe for automated a11y scanning and manual keyboard tests for dialogs and forms.

title: Cross-node vault transfers
description:
MoveChunk/CopyRecords only work for local vaults — when the destination is on another node, they fail with ErrVaultNotFound. This means (1) changing a vault's NodeID in config orphans data on the old node, and (2) retention migrate action can't target vaults on remote nodes.

Fix: when the destination vault isn't registered locally, fall back to shipping chunks over the cluster transport via ImportRecords (or a dedicated streaming RPC). Wire into ApplyConfig vault reconciliation so node reassignment triggers an automatic migration instead of a silent teardown. Retention migrate gets cross-node support for free.

Existing infrastructure: ExportVault/ImportRecords RPCs, MigrateVault three-phase protocol, record-forwarder cluster transport.

title: Volume histogram caps at 1M records, producing misleading single-bar display
description:
## Problem

`timechartScanDirect` in `query/histogram.go:172` hard-caps at 1,000,000 records. When data exceeds this, two things go wrong:

1. **Silent truncation**: The total shows exactly "1,000,000 records" with no indication it's capped.
2. **Misleading histogram**: The scan iterates chronologically and fills the first bucket(s) to the cap, then stops. The histogram shows all records concentrated in one bar (~18s of a 30m window), when data actually spans the full range.

## Reproduction

1. Run chatterbox at high throughput for several minutes
2. Query `last=30m reverse=true`
3. Histogram shows one bar with 1,000,000 records; the rest of the time range is empty

## Root Cause

```go
// histogram.go:172-188
func timechartScanDirect(...) error {
    const maxScan = 1_000_000
    scanned := 0
    for rec, err := range records {
        // ...
        scanned++
        if scanned >= maxScan {
            break  // silently stops, remaining time range is never scanned
        }
    }
}
```

The `timechartScanPreOps` path (with pipe operators) has no such cap.

## Possible Approaches

- **Chunk-level approximation**: Use idx metadata (record count + timestamp bounds per chunk) to build an approximate histogram without scanning every record. O(chunks) instead of O(records).
- **Sampling**: Estimate total records, compute a stride, scan every Nth record.
- **At minimum**: Surface a "capped" indicator in the response so the UI can show "1M+ (approximate)" instead of a precise-looking "1,000,000 records".

title: Histogram stacked bars have rounded corners on all segments
description: After the AttrScanner per-bucket sampling fix (gastrolog-3uxo3), stacked histogram bars show rounded top corners on every segment instead of only the topmost one. borderRadius [2,2,0,0] was applied uniformly. Fixed by computing the topmost non-zero segment per bucket and only rounding that one.

title: Automated vault migration on node reassignment
description: When a vault's NodeID is changed in config, automatically migrate all existing data from the old node to the new one. The new node should begin receiving new records immediately (via the normal ingestion/forwarding path), while the old node drains its sealed chunks to the new node in the background via ForwardMigrateRecords. This differs from local MigrateVault (which freezes the source) — the destination is already live and accepting writes. The old node seals its active chunk, transfers all sealed chunks, then cleans up. gastrolog-4egsn added a detection warning for this scenario; this issue replaces the warning with the actual migration.

title: Vault paths should resolve relative to node home, not process CWD
description:
When running multiple nodes on the same machine, vault directories like `data/vaults/champion-tiger` resolve relative to the process working directory. All nodes share the same CWD, so they collide on the same absolute path — node4 gets `vault directory is locked by another process` when trying to create a vault that node1 already holds open.

This breaks any multi-node-on-same-machine setup, including the new automated drain (vault reassignment). The destination node can't create the vault because the source node still has the directory locked.

**Fix:** Relative vault paths should resolve relative to each node's `--home` directory instead of CWD. Each node already has a separate home (Raft state, etc.), so `data/vaults/X` would become `<node-home>/data/vaults/X` on each node, avoiding collisions.

**Secondary concern:** During drain, `SealActive` schedules async compress/index-build jobs before the drain worker runs. After `finishDrain` closes the chunk manager, those jobs may try to access a closed manager. Worth investigating as a potential crash vector.

title: Vault timeline: use Gantt-style swimlanes for overlapping chunks
description: The vault inspector timeline renders all chunk segments on a single horizontal line. When chunks overlap in time (common during concurrent ingestion or after migration), the bars stack on top of each other and become unreadable. Switch to a Gantt-chart layout where each chunk gets its own row, making temporal overlaps visually clear.

title: GetStats and pipeline queries don't fan out to remote nodes
description:
When vaults are assigned to remote nodes, `GetStats` returns `totalBytes: 0` and pipeline queries like `timechart` produce 0-count buckets — while the main `Search` RPC correctly fans out and returns results. This causes the UI to show "0.0 MB STORAGE" in the header bar and an empty volume histogram despite search results being present.

Confirmed by moving a vault back to the local node — the histogram immediately started showing data again.

Both `GetStats` and pipeline query execution need the same cross-node fan-out that `Search` already has.

Discovered during gastrolog-5xs8.

title: Lightweight cross-node volume histogram fan-out
description:
Replace the current histogram implementation (which goes through the full pipeline/timechart path) with a lightweight fan-out that returns histogram data as part of every ForwardSearchResponse.

## Design

Each node computes a `map[string][]int64` — level name → bucket counts — as a side effect of scanning records during search. Records without a level attribute are counted under a special `"other"` bucket. The coordinator sums matching level keys across all nodes.

This means:
- **No records cross the wire** for histogram data — just bucket counts
- **No deduplication needed** — slight overcounting from route fan-out is acceptable for a visual heatmap signal
- **Zero extra round trips** — histogram data piggybacks on the existing ForwardSearchResponse
- **Dynamic levels** — new levels appearing on any node just create a new key in the map

## Key decisions

- Histogram groups by level attribute, with `"other"` for records missing it
- Accuracy tradeoff: approximate counts are fine (the histogram indicates "something is happening", not exact volumes)
- Deduplication for exact queries is handled separately by an explicit `| dedup` operator (see related issue)

title: Optimize matchesSingleToken to use IterTokens with early exit
description:
matchesSingleToken (scanner.go:1192) allocates a full []string via tokenizer.Tokens() on every call, even though it only checks for a single token. This is called once per record per PredToken predicate during query evaluation — potentially thousands of times per search on unsealed chunks or after index narrowing.

Benchmark shows 278 ns / 10 allocs per call. Switching to IterTokens with early exit on first match would drop to ~0 allocs and cut latency significantly.

Fix: replace Tokens() + slices.Contains with IterTokens callback that returns false (stop) on match. Same optimization applies to matchesTokens which has the same pattern.

title: Replace automatic dedup with explicit | dedup pipeline operator
description:
Currently, every merged search stream applies `ingestDedup` automatically, comparing adjacent `(ingest_ts, ingester_id)` pairs. This has several problems:

1. **Performance tax on every query.** Most deployments don't route records to multiple vaults — the dedup check runs for nothing.
2. **Subtly broken.** `ingestDedup` only catches *adjacent* duplicates in sorted order. Non-adjacent copies from different vaults slip through, so the guarantee is already approximate.
3. **Blocks efficient fan-out.** The histogram, pipeline fan-out, and merge paths all have to account for dedup, adding complexity to every distributed code path.

## Proposal

- Remove automatic `ingestDedup` from `mergeAndStream` and related paths.
- Add a `| dedup` pipeline operator that users opt into when they know their routing config produces duplicates.
- The operator should do proper set-based dedup (not just adjacent-pair), keyed on `(ingest_ts, ingester_id)` or configurable fields.
- Make it visible in the explain plan like any other operator.

## Impact

- Simplifies all distributed merge paths
- Makes the dedup cost explicit and visible
- Enables the lightweight histogram fan-out (gastrolog-280mu) without worrying about overcounting
- Users who need dedup get a *better* version (set-based vs adjacent-only)

title: Histogram ignores query filter — shows all severity levels even when level= filter is active
description: When clicking a severity segment in the histogram (e.g. error), it adds level=error to the query but the histogram still shows all severity levels. ComputeHistogram only uses the time range and vault= predicates — it completely ignores BoolExpr. Since per-level group counts are already computed, the fix is to extract level= predicates from the query and mask GroupCounts to only include matching levels, then recompute the total count as the sum of matching groups.

title: Merge heap and stream interleaving sort on IngestTS instead of WriteTS
description:
The chunk scanning, binary search, time range selection, and chunk filtering all operate on WriteTS — it's the canonical ordering timestamp, monotonic within chunks and available before indexing. But the multi-chunk merge heap (merge.go), the local+remote interleaving (mergeAndStream/drainRemoteUntil in server/query.go), and follow polling all sort on IngestTS instead.

This means:
- Chunks are scanned in WriteTS order (idx.log iteration order)
- But multi-chunk merge re-orders records by IngestTS
- And remote+local interleaving also uses IngestTS

For most records WriteTS ≈ IngestTS so this is invisible, but for late-arriving, backfilled, or cross-ingester records, the merged output can be disordered relative to WriteTS, which is what the user's calendar/time range selection filters on.

Affected code:
- backend/internal/query/merge.go: mergeHeap and mergeHeapReverse Less() use rec.IngestTS
- backend/internal/server/query.go: drainRemoteUntil uses GetIngestTs(), mergeAndStream uses rec.IngestTS as cutoff
- backend/internal/query/search.go: follow polling sorts pending records by IngestTS

All of these should use WriteTS for consistency with the rest of the system.

title: CPU percentage wildly inflated due to shared singleton tracker
description: Multiple callers (GetStats, GetClusterStatus, stats broadcaster) all call CPUPercent() on a shared singleton cpuTracker that resets its measurement baseline on every call. When two callers fire close together, the second one measures over a tiny window (milliseconds) where a single syscall spike dominates, producing readings of 3-5% on a system actually using <1%. Header bar and inspector show different values for the same node at the same time. Fix: enforce a minimum 2-second window between baseline resets so all callers get the same stable reading.

title: Follow mode broken in cross-node cluster (only polls local vaults)
description:
Follow mode stays empty when the vault is on a different node than the one the user is connected to. The Follow handler only calls eng.Follow() which polls local vaults via engine.listVaults(). There is no cross-node fan-out — unlike regular search which has collectRemote().

Repro: Create an ingester on node A, vault on node B, route between them. Connect to node A. Normal search works, follow shows nothing.

Fix: Add a streaming ForwardFollow cluster RPC. The coordinator fans out to remote nodes that own matching vaults, each running eng.Follow() locally and streaming records back. The coordinator merges local and remote follow streams by WriteTS order.

title: Audit and fix all cross-node operations; add cluster-aware review agent
description:
Features have repeatedly been implemented for single-node only, causing subtle bugs in multi-node deployments. This is a systemic problem requiring both a one-time audit and permanent guardrails.

Scope:

1. Audit all RPC endpoints and pipeline operators — identify every operation that queries, mutates, or aggregates data and verify it fans out to all cluster nodes (or correctly delegates to the leader). Document which operations are node-local by design vs. cluster-wide.

2. Fix any operations that silently ignore remote nodes — common failure modes:
   - Query/search only scanning local vaults
   - Stats/metrics only reporting local state
   - Pipeline operators (dedup, stats, head, etc.) only seeing local records
   - Field discovery / autocomplete limited to local data
   - Jobs, config, or admin operations not forwarding to leader

3. Add multi-node integration tests — create a test harness that runs 2-3 in-process nodes in a single test and validates fan-out, aggregation, and leader forwarding. Every cross-node fix gets a test.

4. Create .claude/agents/cross-node-review.md — a persistent custom sub-agent that can be invoked on any new feature to check whether it correctly handles multi-node scenarios. Prevents this class of bug from recurring.

title: GetJob RPC only queries local node
description: GetJob returns 404 for jobs running on remote nodes. Only checks local scheduler, does not fall back to PeerJobsProvider. Fix: check peerJobs when local lookup fails.

title: Non-distributive aggregations produce wrong results in multi-node clusters
description: avg(), dcount(), median(), first(), last(), values() pipeline functions produce incorrect results in multi-node deployments. avg() sums averages across nodes (mathematically wrong). dcount/median/first/last/values are completely unhandled by merge code (rows get concatenated instead of aggregated). Fix: detect pipelines with non-distributive aggregations and route through searchPipelineGlobal to gather raw records on coordinator before running the pipeline once.

title: Add multi-node integration test harness
description: No tests exercise cross-node behavior. Create a test harness that runs 2-3 in-process nodes and validates: search fan-out, pipeline aggregation merge, job visibility, follow streaming, pagination across nodes. Every cross-node fix in this epic must have a corresponding test.

title: Vault admin RPCs don't forward to remote nodes
description: ListChunks, GetIndexes, and ValidateVault use remoteNodeForVault + RemoteVaultForwarder to forward to the owning node. But GetChunk, AnalyzeChunk, SealVault, and ReindexVault only queried the local orchestrator and returned 404 for remote vaults. Fixed by adding remoteNodeForVault checks and Forward RPCs following the existing ListChunks/GetIndexes/ValidateVault pattern.

title: ListIngesters and GetIngesterStatus show incomplete data for remote ingesters
description: ListIngesters shows Running=false for all remote ingesters. GetIngesterStatus shows zero stats (MessagesIngested, Errors, BytesIngested) for remote ingesters. The broadcast system already carries ingester stats in NodeStats.Ingesters but ListIngesters/GetIngesterStatus don't consume this data. Fix: use peer broadcast ingester stats to populate Running and stats fields for remote ingesters.

title: GetVault returns 404 for remote vaults
description: ListVaults correctly includes remote vaults, but GetVault (vault_info.go) only queries the local orchestrator via getVaultInfo. Navigating directly to a remote vault detail page returns 404. Fix: fall back to config store + peer broadcast stats for remote vaults, similar to how ListVaults constructs remote vault entries.

title: Audit all backend features for multi-node test coverage
description:
Too many bugs have shipped because features were only tested in single-node mode and silently broke in cluster configurations. Route stats returning zeros, GetFields returning empty results, and similar issues all stem from the same root cause: missing multi-node test coverage.

This issue tracks a systematic audit of EVERY backend feature to ensure it is tested using the multi-node harness (multinode_test.go). Nothing is exempt.

## Scope

### RPCs (every service, every method)
- ConfigService: GetConfig, PutVault, PutFilter, PutRoute, PutRotationPolicy, PutRetentionPolicy, GetRouteStats, ListIngesters, GetIngesterStatus, WatchConfig, GenerateName, GetIngesterDefaults, etc.
- QueryService: Search, Follow, Explain, GetRecordContext, GetFields
- VaultService: GetVault, ListChunks, GetChunk, AnalyzeChunk, SealVault, ReindexVault, ValidateVault, MergeVaults, ImportRecords
- LifecycleService: GetClusterStatus, GetSettings, PutSettings, SetNodeSuffrage
- JobService: GetJob, WatchJobs

### Features beyond RPCs
- Routing: records ingested on node A reaching vaults on node B via ForwardRecords
- Streaming: Follow (tail -f) across nodes — local + remote streams merged correctly
- Query fan-out: Search, Explain, pipeline aggregation (stats, timechart, avg) across nodes
- Record forwarding: cross-node record delivery, ForwardRecords path
- Config propagation: WatchConfig stream firing on all nodes after Raft commit
- Ingester lifecycle: ingesters running on remote nodes, stats visible from coordinator
- Job visibility: jobs running on remote nodes visible via GetJob/WatchJobs from coordinator
- Route stats: aggregated counters from all nodes (already fixed, verify coverage)
- Vault operations: seal, reindex, validate, merge — all forwarded to correct node
- Retention/rotation: policies applied on the node owning the vault

### Methodology
- For each feature, verify a multi-node test exists using setupMultiNode with a coordinator-only node (no local vault) querying remote data nodes
- If missing, add a test. If the harness lacks support (e.g. missing client type, missing peer provider), extend it
- The coordinator-without-vault topology is critical — it catches the works-locally-breaks-in-cluster class of bugs

### Definition of done
Every RPC and feature listed above has at least one multi-node test that would fail if cross-node support were removed.

title: Per-route stats in routes inspector
description:
The routes inspector page currently shows global stats (ingested/routed/dropped) and per-vault delivery counts. It should also show per-route stats — how many records each route matched — so users can see which routes are active and how traffic distributes across them.

Requires:
1. Track route ID alongside vault ID during FilterSet.MatchWithNode()
2. New per-route counters in the orchestrator (matched/forwarded per route)
3. Add per-route stats to GetRouteStatsResponse proto and cluster broadcast
4. Frontend: per-route table in RouteStatsView.tsx showing route name, filter, matched count

title: Cross-node query forwarding fails for values with glob metacharacters
description: When a KV query value contains characters like `*`, `?`, or `[`, cross-node query forwarding fails because `PredicateExpr.String()` serializes the value without quoting. The remote node re-parses the expression and interprets the metacharacters as glob syntax, producing errors like: `parse error at position 83: '*' must be followed by '='`. Example: `request.headers.authorization="Bearer ***"` returns 0 results on cross-node queries but works fine locally. Fix: quote values in `String()` output when they contain non-bareword characters, while preserving intentional glob patterns.

title: File upload and distribution for lookup tables
description: General file upload mechanism for lookup table data (MMDB, CSV, JSON, SQLite). Files are uploaded to one node and distributed to all cluster nodes via a separate channel (not Raft). Only file metadata (name, hash, size) is stored in the Raft log. Needed as foundation for the planned lookup table types under gastrolog-55wp.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
## Design

### Storage
- Files stored at `<home>/lookups/<file-id>/<filename>`
- File metadata in Raft: id, name, sha256, size, upload time
- No binary data in Raft — only the manifest entry

### Upload Flow
1. HTTP multipart upload endpoint receives file
2. Write to local `<home>/lookups/` with content-hash verification
3. Commit metadata to Raft (so all nodes learn about the file)
4. Stream file to all peers via new cluster gRPC RPC (simple byte streaming, similar to ForwardImportRecords)

### Peer Distribution
- New `ForwardFile` cluster RPC — client-streaming byte chunks
- Receiving node writes to its own `<home>/lookups/` directory
- SHA256 verified on both ends

### Startup Reconciliation
- On boot, each node compares local `lookups/` against Raft manifest
- Missing files are pulled from any peer that has them
- Ensures nodes that were offline during upload catch up

### Deletion
- Delete removes Raft manifest entry + local file on all nodes
- Peers clean up on next config notification

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
### Clarification: Lookup files are entities

- Each lookup file gets a UUIDv7 `file_id` assigned on upload
- Raft manifest is keyed by `file_id`
- Lookup table configs reference files by `file_id`, not by filesystem path
- Storage path: `<home>/lookups/<file_id>/<original_filename>`
- This decouples file identity from filesystem layout and allows lookup tables to be reconfigured without re-uploading

title: Migrate MMDB to lookup file management system
description:
The MaxMind GeoLite2 databases (GeoLite2-City.mmdb, GeoLite2-ASN.mmdb) currently download to a flat `<home>/lookups/` directory and aren't tracked as lookup file entities. This means they don't replicate to other cluster nodes.

## Two aspects

### 1. Auto-download
- `manageMaxMindJob` downloads MMDB files to `<home>/lookups/` (flat path) on one node only
- Files aren't registered in Raft, so they don't distribute to peers
- After migration: downloaded files registered as lookup file entities via `PutLookupFile`, stored under `<home>/lookups/<file_id>/`, auto-distributed to all cluster nodes via FSM notification

### 2. File override
- Users should be able to manually upload their own MMDB files (e.g. commercial GeoIP2 databases) via the upload endpoint or a future UI
- A manual upload should override the auto-downloaded version
- `resolveGeoDBPaths` should prefer the most recently uploaded MMDB by name, regardless of whether it was auto-downloaded or manually uploaded

## Migration
- Existing single-node setups with MMDB files in the flat directory should auto-migrate on startup (register as lookup file entities)
- The lookup file infrastructure (gastrolog-5y487) is already in place: upload, Raft metadata, cluster distribution, reconciliation

title: CLI: add missing commands for routes, managed files, jobs, vault ops, and cluster management
description:
The CLI has fallen behind the backend feature set. Several RPCs that are fully functional via the UI have no CLI equivalent.

## High priority (core workflow)

- **Routes** — `route list`, `route get`, `route create`, `route delete` (PutRoute/DeleteRoute exist, zero CLI coverage)
- **Managed files** — `file list`, `file upload`, `file delete` (new managed file system has no CLI at all)
- **Jobs** — `job list`, `job get`, `job watch` (ListJobs/GetJob/WatchJobs RPCs exist)

## Medium priority (operational)

- **Vault operations** — `vault seal`, `vault reindex`, `vault migrate` (SealVault/ReindexVault/MigrateVault RPCs exist)
- **Ingester status** — `ingester status <name>` (GetIngesterStatus RPC exists, CLI only has list)

## Lower priority (cluster admin)

- **Cluster** — `cluster status`, `node remove`, `node promote/demote`, `shutdown` (all RPCs exist)

title: Fix premature routing stats increment and silent write errors
description:
Two issues found in the ingest pipeline:

1. Route stats (vs.Matched, rs.Matched, routeStats.Routed) increment BEFORE appendLocal/forwardRemote, so they report delivery even when the write fails.

2. writeLoop swallows errors from ingest() — only tracks them in per-ingester stats (which requires a valid ingester ID), never logs them. This makes write failures invisible.

Additionally investigating: records not reaching chunk manager after cluster restart (intermittent). Routing stats show delivery but vault has no new records.

title: Forward records fail during startup causing silent 2-minute buffering
description: After cluster restart, the ForwardRecords gRPC handler is registered (wireClusterForwarding at app.go:156) before the orchestrator has vaults (startOrchestrator at app.go:164). Forwarded records hit orch.Append → ErrVaultNotFound, the sending node's forwarder enters exponential backoff (1s→30s max), and records silently buffer in a 10,000-entry channel. Routing stats (Matched/Forwarded) keep counting up because Forward() is non-blocking, making it look like everything works. After ~2 minutes the backoff resolves and records flush in a burst. Fix: add a readiness gate so the ForwardRecords handler blocks (not fails) until the orchestrator is ready.

title: Intermittent forwarded record loss on cluster restart
description: Records forwarded from ingester node to vault node are silently lost intermittently on cluster restart. 30k records routed, 0 dropped, but vault record count doesn't grow. No error or buffer-full logs. Root cause unknown - the forwarding path has an observability gap: no INFO-level logging on the receiving side for ForwardRecords RPCs.

title: Channel pressure watchdog with threshold-crossing alerts
description: Add periodic channel utilization monitoring to all important channels. Logs once when crossing above a threshold (e.g. 90%) and once when dropping back below. Runs every ~1 second to catch spikes. Replaces the less useful per-10k stats logging. Each watchdog lives in the subsystem that owns the channel.

title: Unify local and remote node stats layout in inspector
description: Local node used a different stats component (LocalSystemStats with rich vertical layout) than remote nodes (CompactView with 2-column grid). Both now use the same CompactView via gossip-broadcast NodeStats. Dead code removed.

title: Top bar stats: aggregate across all nodes with per-node tooltip breakdown
description: The top bar currently shows CPU, Memory, and Storage for the local node only. In a cluster, this is misleading — it ignores other nodes entirely. Change to: sum/aggregate stats across all cluster nodes, and show a tooltip on hover with the per-node breakdown (node name + value). Single-node mode stays unchanged (no tooltip needed). The data is already available via gossip-broadcast NodeStats (same source as the inspector's ClusterSummaryView).

title: Evaluate gossip protocol for stats dissemination
description: Current stats broadcasting uses all-to-all push (every node sends to every peer each tick). This is O(N²) messages per round — fine for small clusters but won't scale. Evaluate switching to a proper gossip protocol (e.g. HashiCorp memberlist/SWIM) where each node contacts a fixed number of random peers per round, achieving O(N log N) convergence with constant per-node bandwidth. Consider: membership detection, failure handling, and whether to replace just stats broadcast or also fold in Raft membership discovery.

title: Separate MaxMind auto-download from lookup settings
description: MaxMind auto-download is a background job that fetches MMDB files — it's not a lookup entity. It currently lives in the Lookups settings page and shares the lookup config namespace, but it has no registry name and doesn't participate in the lookup interface. It should be modeled as a file provider / auto-downloader, not lumped in with lookup configuration.

title: Managed files and MaxMind config lost on Raft snapshot restore
description: FSM.Restore() skips managed files when rebuilding state from a snapshot, so uploaded files disappear from the manifest after cluster restart. Also missing MaxMind from ServerSettings serialization in BuildSnapshot/RestoreSnapshot.

title: Help: break out visualization docs and document operator types
description: Break visualization help into a separate sub-topic with thorough per-operator docs (requirements, examples). Add operator type classification (streaming, short-circuit, materializing, coordinator-only, visualization) to pipeline help. Document timechart performance advantage over stats.

title: Document quorum loss behavior and surface read-only state in UI
description:
A cluster that loses quorum (e.g. a 2-node cluster losing one node) becomes read-only: searches, follows, and config reads all work, but config mutations fail. This needs to be:

1. **Documented** in the clustering help — explain quorum requirements (2N+1 for N fault tolerance), warn about 2-node fragility, and clarify that reads continue without quorum.

2. **Surfaced in the UI** — when the cluster has no Raft leader, show a visual indicator (banner or status badge) that the cluster is in read-only/degraded mode. Settings forms should disable save buttons or show a clear error rather than silently timing out.

title: Research: CLI recover command for quorum-loss recovery
description:
Investigate adding a `gastrolog recover` CLI command for recovering from permanent quorum loss (e.g. a 2-node cluster that loses one node).

The surviving node has all config in its Raft snapshot but can't write because it can't form quorum. Reads still work. Recovery needs to:

1. Call hashicorp/raft `RecoverCluster()` to force a new single-node cluster from the surviving node's state.
2. Re-scope orphaned ingesters (pointing at dead node's ID) to the surviving node.
3. Re-scope orphaned vaults to the surviving node — vault data is just files on disk, so if the directory is still accessible (network storage, moved files), pointing a new vault at it recovers the data.
4. Flag vaults whose directories aren't reachable.

Open questions:
- Should this be fully automatic or interactive (let user choose per-item)?
- Should routes referencing orphaned vaults be updated automatically?
- How to handle the case where the dead node comes back online with stale state?
- Should we warn users about 2-node fragility in the clustering help (see gastrolog-1xxu8)?

title: Delete vault should optionally preserve data files
description:
Deleting a file vault unconditionally runs `os.RemoveAll` on the vault directory, destroying all chunk data. There's no way to delete the config while keeping the data on disk.

This matters for recovery scenarios (re-pointing a new vault at the old directory) and for users who want to reconfigure a vault without losing data.

Changes needed:
- Add a `delete_data` bool field to the `DeleteVault` RPC request.
- Only run `os.RemoveAll` in the dispatcher when `delete_data` is true.
- UI: add a confirmation dialog with a checkbox like "Also delete data files on disk" (default unchecked).
- Pass the flag through the Raft command so the dispatcher on the owning node knows whether to delete.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text: The delete-data option only applies to file vaults. Memory vaults have no directory — the UI should hide the checkbox when deleting a memory vault.

title: Fix Docker Compose example: use 3-node cluster instead of 2-node
description: The multi-node Docker Compose example in README.md shows a 2-node cluster with 2 voters, which is the worst possible topology — it provides zero fault tolerance (quorum of 2 requires both nodes online). Change to a 3-node example (3 voters) to match the recommended production topology and align with the quorum documentation in the clustering help page.

title: Settings push updates not triggering for some newer pages
description: Creating or deleting entities on one of the newer settings pages (possibly Lookups or another recently added page) doesn't immediately reflect in the UI via the WatchConfig push stream. Other settings pages update instantly through the config push mechanism. Need to identify which page(s) are affected — likely missing a configSignal.Notify() call in the FSM dispatcher for the relevant mutation, or the frontend hook isn't invalidating the right query key on WatchConfig events.

title: Enhance gastrolog prime for machine-readable AI agent consumption
description: Make gastrolog prime usable by AI agents for planning and execution. Add --format json output with versioned schema containing resource definitions, ingester type specs, dependency rules, task workflows, command metadata, error model, and validation constraints. Keep human-readable output as default.

title: Add CLI login command for remote authentication
description: There is no way to obtain a JWT from the CLI. Add `gastrolog config login --username X --password Y` that authenticates against the server and prints a JWT to stdout. This enables remote CLI usage and AI agent workflows without needing to extract tokens from the browser. The unix socket bypasses auth for local access, but remote access (--addr) currently requires manually obtaining a token.

title: Refactor oversized functions and components
description:
Several functions and components have grown too large and need splitting:

**Backend:**
- `TestStore` in `backend/internal/config/storetest/storetest.go` — cognitive complexity 523 (SonarQube limit: 15). Single conformance test function with 263 locations. Split into sub-functions by entity type (vaults, ingesters, filters, routes, policies, etc.).

**Frontend (react-doctor warnings):**
- `SetupWizard` (317 lines) — extract logical sections into focused components
- `QueryBar` (129+ lines) — 3 setState calls in a single useEffect, consider useReducer
- `ServiceSettings` (120+ lines)
- `VaultsSettings` — 7 useState calls, consider useReducer
- `CertificatesSettings` (111+ lines)
- `HistogramChart` (146+ lines)
- `RoutesSettings` — 7 useState calls, consider useReducer
- `HelpDialog` — many useState calls
- `LookupsSettings` — many useState calls

Low priority. None of these are bugs, but they make the codebase harder to maintain.

title: Add --vaults flag to override default vault storage directory
description:
Add a --vaults global or server flag that overrides where vault data is stored on disk. Currently vaults are stored under --home, but in containerized deployments (Docker) it's common to mount a separate volume for data storage while keeping config/state in the default home directory. This flag would let users point vault storage at a dedicated mount point without relocating the entire home directory.

Update the Dockerfile to use --vaults with a dedicated volume mount path.

title: Vault form allows saving retention rules with no policy selected
description:
The vault create/edit form lets you add a retention rule row and click Create/Save without selecting a policy in the dropdown. This sends an empty retention policy ID to the backend, which rejects it with 'invalid retention policy ID: invalid UUID length: 0'.

The form should either:
- Disable Create/Save when any retention rule has no policy selected
- Or remove empty retention rules before submitting

title: Docker Compose integration test environment for cloud storage vaults
description:
Create a docker-compose.yml (or extend the existing ingester one) that brings up local emulators for all three cloud storage backends:

- **MinIO** — S3-compatible object storage
- **Azurite** — Azure Blob Storage emulator (official Microsoft image)
- **fake-gcs-server** — GCS emulator (fsouza/fake-gcs-server)

Each emulator should have pre-created buckets/containers ready for vault use. The compose environment should allow end-to-end testing of chunk upload, metadata tagging, listing, and query-time filtering against all three providers without cloud credentials or egress costs.

title: BlobStore interface and S3/Azure/GCS implementations
description:
Define the BlobStore abstraction in a new `backend/internal/blobstore` package with implementations for S3, Azure Blob, and GCS. Each implementation wraps the provider's native SDK and returns custom metadata in List results.

Interface: Upload, Download, Delete, List (returns BlobInfo with Key, Size, Metadata).

SDKs:
- S3: github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2
- Azure: github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob
- GCS: cloud.google.com/go/storage

Include integration tests that run against the cloud-storage compose emulators (MinIO, Azurite, fake-gcs-server). Tests should cover upload with metadata, list with metadata filtering, download, and delete.

title: Cloud chunk blob format
description:
Design and implement the single-blob format for cloud-archived chunks. Each chunk becomes one object in the bucket.

Format:
- Fixed header: magic bytes, format version, metadata (time range, record count, vault ID, chunk ID, attribute dictionary size)
- Attribute dictionary: shared string table for repeated keys
- Record frames: length-prefixed records with ingest timestamp + attributes + raw log
- Whole-blob zstd compression

The format must support streaming reads (no random access required — cloud queries scan the full blob). Include a writer (for upload) and reader (for query-time scan).

title: Cloud chunk manager (sealed-only vault type)
description:
Implement a chunk.ChunkManager for the `cloud` vault type. Unlike file/memory managers, this one is sealed-only — no Append().

Responsibilities:
- Wraps BlobStore for storage operations
- Converts sealed file-vault chunks to cloud blob format on upload
- Lists available chunks via BlobStore.List with metadata
- Downloads and decodes blobs for query-time scanning
- Registers as vault type `cloud` in the chunk manager factory

The query engine needs to work with cloud chunks — likely a scan-only path since there are no on-disk indexes.

title: Archive retention action for cloud vault migration
description:
Add an `archive` retention action alongside the existing `expire` action. When a sealed chunk matches a retention rule with action `archive`:

1. Convert the chunk to cloud blob format
2. Upload to the target cloud vault via BlobStore
3. Delete the local chunk after successful upload

This requires:
- New retention action type in config proto and store
- Retention rule references a destination cloud vault ID
- Orchestrator scheduler handles archive jobs (similar to expire but uploads first)
- Frontend retention rule editor supports the archive action with a vault destination picker

title: Cloud vault settings UI
description:
Add the `cloud` vault type to the frontend settings:

- Provider selector (S3, Azure Blob, GCS)
- Provider-specific fields: bucket, region, endpoint (optional), access key/secret (S3), connection string (Azure), credentials JSON (GCS)
- Key prefix field (optional)
- Test connection button (list bucket to verify credentials)
- Help page for cloud vault type

Cloud vaults should be visually distinct from file/memory vaults (e.g. a cloud icon or badge) since they're sealed-only and behave differently.

title: Audit and sort all user-facing lists in the frontend
description:
Go through every dropdown, list, and selectable collection in the frontend and ensure they are alphabetically sorted. This includes (but is not limited to):

- Vault dropdowns (creation type picker, retention rule destination picker)
- Policy dropdowns (retention rule policy picker)
- Ingester lists and dropdowns
- Node selectors
- Any other SelectInput, combobox, or rendered list that displays user-created entities

This has been a recurring issue — unsorted lists keep slipping through because there's no consistent pattern enforced. The fix should also establish a convention (e.g. a shared helper or a note in CLAUDE.md) so future lists are sorted by default.

title: Validate duplicate retention policy in migrate rules
description:
When the same retention policy appears in more than one 'migrate' rule on a vault, only the first rule executes (per the processed-map dedup in retention.go). The remaining rules are dead — the chunk is moved by the first rule and no longer exists in the source vault for subsequent rules.

Add frontend validation: warn or prevent adding multiple migrate rules that reference the same retention policy on the same vault. This could be:
- Disable Save with an inline warning when duplicate policy+migrate combinations are detected
- Or filter out already-used policies from the dropdown when action is 'migrate'

Also consider whether the backend should reject this configuration in PutVault validation.

title: Retention-triggered migration via routes instead of direct vault destination
description:
Currently, retention rules on a vault specify a destination vault directly (action=migrate, destination=vault_id). This has several problems:

1. Only one migrate rule fires per chunk (dedup in retention sweep), so multiple destinations are impossible
2. Fan-out to N cloud vaults requires N file vaults with identical routes
3. The vault owns both 'when to act' and 'where to go', coupling concerns

## Design

### Eject action
Retention rules keep the policy (when) and action type. A new `eject` action replaces `migrate` — instead of specifying a destination vault, it names one or more target routes. The routes handle fan-out, filtering, and destination selection.

### Record-level decomposition
Eject decomposes sealed chunks back into a record stream. This is not a performance tradeoff — it's the general case:
- Cloud vault migrations already decompose via CursorIterator → ImportRecords
- Cross-node transfers already stream records over RPC
- O(1) filesystem rename only applies to file→file same-mount (the exception)

This also establishes shared infrastructure for rechunking (gastrolog-3c0a6).

### Explicit route targeting (not implicit routing)
Ejected records do NOT flow through the live ingestion FilterSet. The eject action explicitly names the target route(s) — no FilterSet evaluation for route selection. This prevents loops where ejected records re-match their original routes.

### Route scoping: ingestion flag
Routes get a boolean `ingestion` flag (default true):
- **ingestion=true**: included in the live FilterSet, cannot be selected as an eject target
- **ingestion=false**: excluded from the live FilterSet, eligible as an eject target

The UI only offers non-ingestion routes in the eject route picker. Backend validation enforces the same constraint.

### Digest pipeline: bypass
Ejected records have already been digested during initial ingestion. The eject path feeds directly into routing, skipping the digest loop.

### Backpressure: priority select
A secondary `ejectCh` channel feeds into the write loop alongside `digestedCh`. Nested select prioritizes live ingestion — ejected records only process when the live channel is empty. Starvation of eject under constant load is acceptable.

### Delivery guarantees
- **Local destinations**: appendLocal() is synchronous under the orchestrator lock.
- **Remote destinations**: Use RemoteTransferrer (synchronous, reliable) instead of RecordForwarder (async, drops).
- **Source chunk deletion**: Only after full cursor drain and all destinations confirm delivery. Failed eject aborts, chunk remains intact, next retention sweep retries.

### Eject flow
1. Retention sweep matches chunk via policy
2. Eject action opens cursor on sealed chunk
3. Records fed through explicitly named route(s) only
4. Each named route applies its own filter and fans out to destination vaults
5. Source chunk deleted after confirmed delivery

## Dependencies
Depends on gastrolog-23pka (WriteTS demotion) — ejected records get fresh WriteTS at the destination, retention clock resets. Part of epic gastrolog-5mzlf.

comments.author: kluzz@radical.org
comments.text:
## Design decision: record-level decomposition, not chunk-level routing
